must_detect.matched_text: The reference “client code 0F00-0000” and “IPO list.xlsx” in the folder tree were legacy test artifacts unrelated to any live offering.

Incident summary for Hang Prosper Holdings Limited (Main Board issuer; internal ref IMS-2025-17) regarding phishing-related mailbox misuse on 28–29 May.
The event concerned shared role mailboxes at PearlGate Securities (HK) Limited (group broker) and Jade Meridian Capital Fund SPC (affiliated fund manager, asset management licensed), with no trading instructions altered.
We stress no MNPI leak: the only deal materials touched were the already-announced heads of terms for the proposed acquisition of Auric Reef Logistics Ltd, duplicating content on the Exchange’s disclosure website dated three months earlier.
A legacy USB drive contained a spreadsheet labelled “Investor KYC” but fields held mock values (e.g., HKID: X000000(0), account: 000-000-000-0000), and no live client files were present.
The compromised credentials belonged to a facilities role account “ops.facility@hangprosper.local” used for building access notifications, not a named individual.
Systems impacted: SkyOffice 365 shared mailbox, one retired endpoint tagged HP-WS-ARCH-019, and an on-prem file share named \\hp-fs01\archive.
One contractor record in an onboarding form combined weak identifiers: employer Harbourline Property Services Limited (outsourced), role “site technician,” work location “Kai Fung Bay depot,” device asset tag HB-NTB-2219, internal staff ID OPS-4432, month-year of birth 1997-04, and a masked payroll account “323-***-**18.”
These fields are fragmented and, except for the masked account and staff code, would not identify the individual alone under the PDPO.
No customer names, national ID scans, or bank instructions were accessed; sample data included generic placeholders such as “Name: A. Sample” and “DOB: 01/01/1900” used for training.
Trade blotter exports viewed contained only end-of-day summaries already in the public domain and did not include any specific, non-public price-sensitive information as contemplated by the SFO.
We informed the Hong Kong securities regulator via the public enquiries channel and filed a precautionary update through the Exchange’s e-submission portal, noting no impact to market operations.
Internally, HR has rotated shared passwords, disabled the “ops.facility” mailbox, and enforced MFA; staff were reminded not to store employment forms on shared drives.
The reference “client code 0F00-0000” and “IPO list.xlsx” in the folder tree were legacy test artifacts unrelated to any live offering.
